model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: UConn enters the tournament as a top-10 team nationally, boasting elite efficiency on both ends of the floor, particularly a top-10 defense that should stifle Furman's 200th-ranked offense. While the Huskies are coming off a disappointing loss to St. John's in the Big East tournament and face uncertainty regarding forward Jaylin Stewart's availability, the talent gap between these two squads is immense. Furman's analytical profile suggests they lack the offensive firepower or defensive discipline to challenge a battle-tested UConn team that has consistently performed at an elite level throughout the season.

reasoningStep.title: UConn vs. Furman Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: UConn is a dominant 2-seed with top-10 efficiency metrics, while Furman is a statistically weak 15-seed ranked outside the top 190 nationally.
evidence: UConn (29-4) ranks 9th in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of +29.0, featuring a top-10 defense.
Furman (22-12) ranks 191st in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of -2.0 and an offensive rank of 200th.
UConn's strength of schedule and performance in the Big East provide a significant talent advantage over the Southern Conference champion.
Despite a recent loss to St. John's and minor injury concerns for Jaylin Stewart, UConn's overall quality remains elite.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026

Current game label: East 2/15
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: UConn (uconn) vs Furman (furman)
Prior committed picks: 11
